summary: A two-story U-shaped wooden building with four tall corner towers and a raised dark maroon (nether) tiled roof capped by light stone trim. Ground-level stone slab pads run around the base. The central footprint opens to an inner courtyard; windows are narrow horizontal bands with glass panes and stone/iron rail accents.

shape.overall_form: U-shaped building with four tall corner towers and a continuous curved roof running along the U
shape.symmetry: approximately bilateral along the central axis (left-right), towers at corners create rough symmetry

materials.name: slab_stone
materials.role: roof/trim caps and base slab pads

elements: four tall corner towers with overhanging capped platforms
dark maroon stepped roof with light stone trim
banded horizontal windows with glass panes
stone slab pads around exterior base
central recessed entrance with stone steps
rebuild_hints: Start with a U-shaped 2-floor wooden rectangle (approx 21x17) leaving a courtyard open in the center.
Place four tall square towers on the exterior corners; towers extend ~10-12 blocks above ground and use vertical log corners with plank faces.
Use dark red (nether_brick) stairs/blocks to form a stepped gable roof following the U curve; cap roof edges with light stone slabs.
Add horizontal bands of glass pane windows separated by thin stone/metal rails on both floors.
Place stone slab pads (single block offset) along the foundation perimeter and a small stone step entry at the front center.
uncertainties: Exact block types for dark roof: looks like nether_brick (brick id used) but could be another dark red block.
Some trim pieces (thin gray band below roof and window rail) might be stonebrick, cobblestone, or iron bars; identified as stonebrick/fence with moderate confidence.
Precise internal floor layout and interior details are not visible; courtyard depth and internal partitions are inferred from exterior only.
Exact dimensions are approximate and rounded to nearest integer based on visible block counts and typical proportions.
